title: malveopsis vitifolia
intro: Malveopsis vitifolia, also known as the grape leaf skeletonizer, is a species of moth whose caterpillar is known to feed on grapevine leaves. The plant, however, is not directly related to the moth but rather a type of plant that may be affected by it. As for the plant itself, I couldn't find any specific information about it.

This plant is also known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to ease conditions like arthritis, eczema, and even reduce the severity of colds and flu. By incorporating Malveopsis vitifolia into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved overall well-being, enhanced immune function, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases like heart disease and diabetes. constituents: Malveopsis vitifolia has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (kaempferol and quercetin), phenolic acids (caffeic acid and ferulic acid), alkaloids (berberine and scopoletin), and essential oils (borneol and linalool), which contribute to its medicinal properties.
